item.title: Apache ShardingSphere企业用户案例：中商慧敏的交易中台架构 | Apache ShardingSphere Enterprise User Case: Zhongshang Huimin’s Transaction Middle Platform Architecture
item.description:
快速消费品（快速消费品）行业已经不能满足当前的发展需求。 传统的只升级供应链末端的方式无法实现业务的高质量发展。 惠民专注于超百万社区超市的订单履行和配送服务，从服务零售终端店向推动快消品全产业链数字化转型升级。

致力于打造B2B2C数据闭环运营体系，为品牌商、经销商、社区超市提供采购、营销、运营、配送全链条的转型升级解决方案。 所有这些承诺都离不开其强大的“中间平台”系统。

引入“中间平台”

惠民从2016年到2017年开始将PHP技术栈转移到Java技术栈，并转向微服务，2018年开始搭建“中间平台”。

其交易中间平台一期项目于2021年初上线，二期项目于2022年3月上线。

由于业务增长和各种变化，惠民推出了强大的“中台”发展战略，以提高效率和降低成本。 采取措施降低系统之间的耦合度，提高可扩展性，在提取业务共性的同时保证低延迟。 同时，根据业务分层，项目还拆分为订单查询系统、报表系统、订单管理后台。

为了在重构过程中协同技术和业务需求，上线过程分为两个阶段：应用系统拆分和数据拆分。

系统拆分

研发团队不得不频繁调整订单管理系统并使其适应多个业务线可能会导致效率低下。 针对这些问题，制定了以下原则对系统进行拆分和重建：

  1. 避免不同业务线的逻辑耦合。 不同的业务线应该相互包容并尽量减少相互影响，以降低逻辑复杂度。
  2. 根据订单生命周期划分职责，确保每个系统独立且具有专属职责。
  3. 读/写拆分。 保证核心功能的稳定性，避免频繁迭代对核心功能的影响。
  4. 引入ElasticSearch是为了解决外部索引问题，减少数据库索引压力。

数据拆分

数据拆分的目的是为了减少单表的维护压力。 当数据量达到千万级时，数据库表的索引和字段维护将对在线环境产生很大的影响。 在拆分表之前，您必须考虑以下问题：

  1. 拆分规则：哪些表是数据密集型的？ 分裂的数量呢？
  2. 数据读写：分表后读写问题如何解决？
  3. 上线解决方案：如何顺利上线？

拆分规则

当我们使用InnoDB引擎进行 DDL 操作来维护阿里云RDS MySQL 时，我们得出以下结果：

当数据量小于 500 万时，执行时间约为几十秒。 超过5000万时，执行时间超过2000秒。

单表拆分上限取决于单表操作对业务的影响。 当然，也有一些解决方案可以解决 DDL 操作导致的表锁问题。 例如，双表同步和表名更改可以将影响降低到几秒钟内。

库表的分割数最好是2的N次方，有利于后面的水平缩放。

技术选择

数据表拆分后，需要解决数据散列和查询问题。 作为SME（中小企业），开发一套分片中间件的成本太高，前期会带来各种风险。 一个合理的解决方案是采用一套开源的分库分库中间件。

项目包括一个JDBC、一个Proxy和Sidecar（Planning），可以独立部署，支持混合部署。 它们都提供了数据横向扩展、分布式事务和分布式治理的功能。

目前，通过插件将数据分片、读写拆分、数据加密、影子库压测以及SQL和协议支持如MySQL 、 PostgreSQL 、 SQL Server 、 Oracle等功能编织到项目中，方便开发者使用定制自己独特的系统。

为什么慧敏选择 Apache ShardingSphere：

  1. 公司采用SpringCloud技术栈，方便集成，降低成本。

  2. 其性能完全可以支撑惠民现有的业务。

目前惠民的服务端技术栈只涉及Java语言，不需要考虑异构场景。 综合考虑灵活性、代码侵入和部署成本，最终选择了ShardingSphere-JDBC的实现。

技术实施

该过程中最复杂的部分实际上是上线部分，而不是人们期望的插件集成部分。

为了实现无感知和回滚，我们将上线流程分为以下几个步骤，如图：

步骤1：处理所有数据需要保证新旧库之间的数据一致性。 由于目前还没有支持数据分片策略的同步工具，所以我们开发了一个数据同步工具来支持分片策略的配置。

第二步：处理增量数据，保持新旧库之间的数据一致性。 我们这里的实现方案是使用开源组件，监听数据库的binlog，将数据库的变化同步到消息队列中，然后数据同步工具监听消息并写入新的库中。

架构方案并不复杂，但在这个过程中非常重视数据的一致性。 增量数据不能丢失，单行数据要按顺序写入，防止出现ABA问题。

第三步：处理读流量的灰度释放。 此时，我们正在编写一个旧库并读取数据库中的一个新库。 在这个过程中，要注意业务对数据一致性的敏感性，因为从老库到新库的延迟是秒级的。 当我们遇到一些需要高一致性的敏感场景时，需要阅读一个旧的库。

当然，灰度方案也可以达到同样的效果。 我们支持回滚策略，将数据从新库写回旧库，然后在一个版本中将所有流量统一转移到新库。

至此，发布上线的全流程已经完成。 由于借助 ShardingSphere-JDBC 中间件重写 SQL 和合并结果完成转换的成本很低，几乎没有代码侵入，上线顺利。

在访问 ShardingSphere-JDBC 方案时，您可能还需要注意一些问题：

  1. 如果 SQL 中没有 shard 列，则会将相同的数据插入到每个 shard 表中。

  2. 此问题已报告，应在不久的将来修复。 此外，还提供了详细的 SQL 示例，其中详细列出了支持的范围。

  3. 为了资源控制，应该限制业务访问的数据库连接数。 可以有效防止一个服务操作占用过多资源，耗尽数据库连接资源，影响其他服务的正常访问。 尤其是在一个数据库实例中有很多子表的情况下，一条没有分片键的逻辑SQL会产生大量的实际SQL，落在同一个库的不同表上。 如果每个实际的 SQL 占用一个单独的连接，那么一个查询无疑会占用太多资源。

从执行效率的角度来看，为每个分片查询维护单独的数据库连接，可以更有效地利用多线程来提高执行效率。 为每个数据库连接构建一个单独的线程，可以同时处理 I/O 消耗，为每个分片维护一个单独的数据库连接，并避免将查询结果数据过早加载到内存中。 单独的数据库连接可以保存对查询结果集的游标位置的引用，并在需要获取相应数据时移动游标。

通过将结果集光标下移的结果合并方法称为流式合并。 它不必将所有结果数据都加载到内存中，可以有效节省内存资源，减少垃圾回收的频率。 如果不能保证每个分片查询都有单独的数据库连接，则需要将当前查询结果集加载到内存中，然后再使用数据库连接来获取下一个分片表的查询结果集。 因此，即使可以使用流式合并，在这种情况下也会退化为内存合并。

具体来说，如果一条SQL在ShardingSphere中分片后需要对一个数据库实例下的200张表进行操作，是创建200个连接并发执行还是创建一个连接依次执行？ 如何在效率和资源控制之间做出选择？

针对上述场景，ShardingSphere 提供了解决方案。 介绍了Connection Mode的概念，分为MEMORY_STRICTLY模式和CONNECTION_STRICTLY模式。

MEMORY_STRICTLY

使用该模式的前提是 ShardingSphere 不限制操作所需的数据库连接数。 如果实际执行的 SQL 需要对数据库实例中的 200 张表进行操作，则为每张表创建一个新的数据库连接，并通过多线程并发处理，以最大限度地提高执行效率。 当 SQL 满足条件时，优先使用流式合并，以防止内存溢出或频繁的垃圾回收。

CONNECTION_STRICTLY

使用这种模式的前提是 ShardingSphere 严格限制了一次操作所需的数据库连接数。 如果实际执行的 SQL 需要对数据库实例中的 200 张表进行操作，则只创建一个数据库连接，并按顺序处理其 200 张表。 如果一个操作中的分片分散在不同的数据库中，仍然使用多线程来处理不同库的操作，但每个库的每个操作只创建一个数据库连接。 这种做法可以防止一个请求消耗过多数据库连接的问题。 该模式一直采用内存合并。

因此，严格控制数据库连接以确保在线系统中的数据库资源可以被更多应用程序使用是一个明智的选择。

我们发现在MEMORY_STRICTLY模式下，由于 MySQL InnoDB 引擎的缓存缓冲区加载策略，操作会变得 I/O 密集型，导致 SQL 超时。 它的解决方案是在不改变数据库资源的情况下再增加一层处理。 如果没有 shard key，则在外部索引中验证 shard key，然后使用 shard key 进行数据库检索。

通过架构重构，可以有效控制单表数据量，大幅降低慢SQL，下降近50%。

结论
我们创造的东西不是固定的时间点。 一旦投入生产，我们的软件将随着使用方式的变化而不断发展。 对于我们创造的大多数东西，我们必须接受这样的事实：一旦软件进入我们的客户手中，我们将不得不做出反应和适应，而不是让它成为一个永不改变的工件。 因此，我们的架构师需要将他们的思维从创造完美的最终产品转变为专注于帮助创建一个可以出现正确系统的框架，并随着我们了解更多而继续发展（Sam Newman，2015：15）。 ” 作者

翟阳

中商惠民电商平台团队软件架构师。

参与0-1中间平台建设过程，主要负责研发建设交易和商品中间平台和搜索平台。



原文:

The FMCG (fast moving consumer-goods) industry can no longer meet current development requirements. The traditional way of only upgrading the end of the supply chain cannot achieve high-quality business development. Huimin focuses on order fulfillment and delivery service for over one million community supermarkets, transforming and upgrading from serving retail terminal stores to promoting the digitization of the whole FMCG industry chain.

It is committed to building a B2B2C data closed-loop operating system, providing brand owners, distributors, and community supermarkets with transformed and upgraded solutions for the whole chain covering purchasing, marketing, operation, and distribution. All these commitments cannot be fulfilled without its powerful “middle platform” system.

Introducing the “middle platform”

Huimin began to transferred PHP technology stack to the Java technology stack, and shifted to microservices from 2016 to 2017, and started to build the “middle platform” in 2018.

The phase-one project of its transaction middle platform went live in early 2021, and phase-two in March 2022.

Owing to business growth and various changes, Huimin launched a strong “middle platform” development strategy to improve efficiency and reduce costs. Measures were taken to reduce the coupling degree between systems, increase scalability, and ensure low latency while extracting business commonalities. At the beginning of 2021, the company finihsed rebuilding the previous order management system (OMS) and launched a packaged business capability project for a transaction middle platform centered on the transaction process of orders, which is divided into the following four core modules: order status flow system, order management system, order fulfillment system and order cost calculation system. At the same time, based on business stratification, the project is also split into an order query system, report system, and order management backend.

To synergize technical and business requirements during the rebuilding process, the go-live process was divided into two phases: application system splitting and data splitting.

System splitting

R&D teams having to frequently adjust order management system and adapt it to multiple business lines may cause inefficiencies. In response to these problems, the following principles were formulated to split and rebuild the system:

  1. Avoid the logic coupling of different lines of business. Different business lines should contain and minimize influence on each other to reduce logic complexity.
  2. Responsibilities are divided according to the order lifecycle to ensure that each system is independent with an exclusive responsibility.
  3. Read/write splitting. Ensure the stability of core functions and avoid the impact of frequent iterations on core functions.
  4. ElasticSearch was introduced to solve the external index problem, and reduce database index pressure.

Data splitting

The purpose of data splitting is to reduce the maintenance pressure of a single table. When the amount of data reaches tens of millions, the index and field maintenance of database tables will have a great impact on the online environment. Before splitting a table, you have to consider the following questions:

  1. Splitting rules: what tables are data-intensive? What about the number of splits?
  2. Data read/write: how to solve the read/write problem after splitting tables?
  3. Go-live solution: how to go live smoothly?

Splitting rules

During a 3 years iteration period, the number of splits = ((amount of data added per month * 36) + Amount of historical data) / maximum amount of data in a single table.

When we use the InnoDB engine for DDL operation to maintain Alibaba Cloud RDS MySQL, we come up with the following results:

When the amount of data is less than 5 million, the execution time is about tens of seconds. Between 5 million and less than 10 million, the execution time is about 500 seconds; between 10 million and less than 50 million, the execution time is about 1000 seconds; and when more than 50 million, the execution time is more than 2000 seconds.

The upper limit for splitting a single table depends on the impact of a single table operation on business. Of course, some solutions can tackle the problem of table lock resulting from DDL operation. For example, dual-table synchronization and changes in table names can reduce the impact to within seconds.

The split number of the library table is preferably 2 to the power of N, which is conducive to later horizontal scaling.

Technology selection

After the data table is split, data hashing and query issues need to be resolved. As a SME (small & medium-sized enterprise), the cost of developing a set of sharding middleware is too high, and it will cause various risks in the early stage. A reasonable solution is to adopt a set of open source database sharding middleware.

Apache ShardingSphere

Apache ShardingSphere is an open source ecosystem that allows you to transform any database into a distributed database system. The project includes a JDBC, a Proxy and Sidecar (Planning), which can be deployed independently and support mixed deployment. They all provide functions of data scale-out, distributed transactions and distributed governance.

Apache ShardingSphere 5.x introduces the concept of Database Plus, and the new version focuses on a plugin oriented architecture. Currently, functions including data sharding, read/write splitting, data encryption, shadow library pressure testing, as well as SQL and protocol support such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle, etc. are woven into the project through plugins, allowing developers to customize their own unique systems.

Why Huimin chose Apache ShardingSphere:

  1. Apache ShardingSphere has a variety of functions and can solve current challenges with strong scalability.

  2. The Apache ShardingSphere community is very active, which means the possibility to find support quickly.

  3. The company adopts the SpringCloud technology stack which is convenient for integration and can lower costs.

  4. Its performance can fully support Huimin’s existing business.

ShardingSphere supports the following 3 modes:

Currently, Huimin’s server-side technology stack only involves Java language, so there is no need to consider heterogeneous scenarios. Taking flexibility, code intrusion and deployment cost into consideration, the implementation of ShardingSphere-JDBC wasfinally selected.

Technology implementation

The most complicated part of the process is actually the go-live part, not the plugin integration part as would someone expect.

To achieve non-perception and rollback, we divided the go-live process into the following steps, as shown in the figure:

Step 1: Processing all the data needs to ensure the data consistency between the old and new libraries. Since there currently is no synchronization tool supporting data sharding policy, we developed a data synchronization tool to support the configuration of the sharding policy.

Step 2: Process incremental data to keep data consistency between the old and new libraries. Our implementation scheme here was to use the open source component, to listen to the binlog of the database and synchronize the database changes to the message queue, and then the data synchronization tool listens to the messages and writes them into the new library.

The architecture scheme is not complicated, but great importance was attached to the data consistency in this process. Incremental data cannot be lost, and single-line data should be written in sequential order to prevent ABA problems.

Step 3: Process the gray-release of read-traffic. At this point, we were writing an old library and reading a new library in the database. In this process, we should paid attention to the sensitivity of business to data consistency, because the delay from an old library to a new library is within seconds. Reading an old library is needed when we come across some sensitive scenarios requiring high consistency.

Step 4: Switch all read-traffic of the application to the new library, maintaining the status of writing the old library and reading the new library.

Step 5: Processing write-traffic didn’t take gray-release scenarios into consideration in order to reduce the complexity of the program. Of course, the grayscale scheme can also achieve the same effect. We supported the rollback strategy by writing data from the new library back to the old library, and then uniformly transferring all traffic to the new library within one release.

So far, the whole process of release and going live has been completed. Due to the low cost of completing the transformation with the help of ShardingSphere-JDBC middleware to rewrite SQL and merge results, there was almost no code intrusion and going live went smoothly.

There are also some issues that you may need to be aware of when accessing the ShardingSphere-JDBC scheme:

  1. ShardingSphere-JDBC supports a default processing for data insertion logic. If there is no shard column in SQL, the same data will be inserted into each shard table.

  2. ShardingSphere-JDBC parses only the logil table of the first SQL for batches of SQL semantic analysis, which results in an error. This problem has been reported and should be fixed in the near future. Additionally, a detailed SQL sample is provided, which lists in detail the scope of support.

  3. ShardingSphere connection mode selection. The number of database connections visited by businesses should be limited for the sake of resource control. It can effectively prevent a service operation from occupying too many resources, which depletes the database connection resources and affects the normal access of other services. Especially in the case of many sub-tables in a database instance, a logical SQL without shard keys will produce a large number of actual SQL that falls on different tables in the same library. If each actual SQL occupies a separate connection, a query will undoubtedly occupy too many resources.

From the perspective of execution efficiency, maintaining a separate database connection for each shard query can make more effective use of multi-threading to improve execution efficiency. Building a separate thread for each database connection allows I/O consumption to be processed concurrently, maintains a separate database connection for each shard, and avoids premature loading of query result data into memory. A separate database connection can hold a reference to the cursor location of the query result set and move the cursor when it needs to fetch the corresponding data.

The result merging method by moving the result set cursor down is called streaming merge. It does not have to load all the result data into memory, which can effectively save memory resources and reduce the frequency of garbage collection. If each shard query cannot be guaranteed to have a separate database connection, the current query result set needs to be loaded into memory before reusing the database connection to obtain the query result set of the next shard table. Therefore, even if streaming merge could be used, it would degenerate into a memory merge in this scenario.

It is a problem for the ShardingSphere execution engine to balance the relationship between the control and protection of database connection resources and saving middleware memory resources by adopting a better merging mode. Specifically, if a SQL needs to operate on 200 tables under a database instance after its sharding in ShardingSphere, whether to create 200 connections and execute them concurrently or create one connection and execute it in sequence? How to choose between efficiency and resource control?

In response to the above scenario, ShardingSphere provides a solution. It introduces the concept of Connection Mode and divides it into MEMORY_STRICTLY mode and CONNECTION_STRICTLY mode.

MEMORY_STRICTLY

The premise of using this pattern is that ShardingSphere does not limit the number of database connections required by an operation. If the actual SQL executed needs to operate on 200 tables in a database instance, a new database connection is created for each table and processed concurrently through multi-threading to maximize the execution efficiency. When SQL meets the conditions, streaming merge is preferred to prevent memory overflow or frequent garbage collection.

CONNECTION_STRICTLY

The premise for using this pattern is that ShardingSphere strictly limits the number of database connections required for an operation. If the actual SQL executed needs to operate on 200 tables in a database instance, only one single database connection is created and its 200 tables are processed sequentially. If shards in an operation are scattered across different databases, multi-threading is still used to handle operations on different libraries, but only one database connection is created for each operation of each library. The practice prevents the problem of consuming too many database connections for one request. This pattern adopts memory merge all the time.

The MEMORY_STRICTLY mode is applicable for OLAP operations and can improve system throughput by relaxing limits on database connections. The CONNECTION_STRICTLY mode applies to OLTP operations, which usually have shard keys and are routed to a single shard. Therefore, it is a wise choice to strictly control database connections to ensure that database resources in an online system can be used by more applications.

We found that in the MEMORY_STRICTLY mode, the operation would become I/O-intensive due to the cache buffer loading strategy of MySQL InnoDB engine, resulting in SQL timeouts. Its solution is to add another layer of processing without changing database resources. If there is no shard key, verify the shard key in the external index, and then use the shard key to perform database retrieval.

ShardingSphere Benefits

1. Performance improvement
Through architecture rebuilding, it can effectively control the amount of single table data, and greatly reduce the slow SQL, down nearly 50%.

2. Save R&D resources and lower cost

The introduction of Apache ShardingSphere does not require the re-development of sharding components, which reduces the R&D cost and lowers the risks.

3. Strong scalability

Apache ShardingSphere has good scalability in terms of data encryption, distributed transactions, shadow library and other aspects.

Conclusion
In his book Building Microservices, Sam Newman writes, “ Our requirements shift more rapidly than they do for people who design and build buildings — as do the tools and techniques at our disposal. The things we create are not fixed points in time. Once launched into production, our software will continue to evolve as the way it is used changes. For most things we create, we have to accept that once the software gets into the hands of our customers we will have to react and adapt, rather than it being a never-changing artifact. Thus, our architects need to shift their thinking away from creating the perfect end product, and instead focus on helping create a framework in which the right systems can emerge, and continue to grow as we learn more (Sam Newman, 2015: 15).” Apache ShardingSphere is doing exactly what the book says, and is definitely on a promising path.

Author

Zhai Yang

Software architect at Zhongshang Huimin E-Commerce platform team.

Participated in the 0–1 process of middle platform construction, mainly responsible for the R&D and building the transaction and commodity middle platform and the search platform.
item.author: apache_shardingsphere

item.title: 我在哪里可以获得资金？ | Where can I get the funds?
item.description:
你好，
我希望你做得很好。

我是 MD Sarfaraj，我是一名软件工程师和博主。
自从姐姐 Zeba Khatoon 不幸去世后，我一直想知道我能为她做些什么。 作为纪念，我建立了 Zeba 基金会以纪念她。

在印度比哈尔邦，许多学生想学习计算机技能，但由于贫困而负担不起。 此外，私人教练中心收取过高的费用。

比哈尔邦政府声称他们正在学校教授计算机技能，但实际上，似乎什么都没有发生。

所以让我向你寻求经济上的帮助，这样我们就可以在农村地区开设计算机中心并向贫困学生教授计算机技能。

请访问网站了解更多信息：
热烈的问候，MD Sarfaraj（软件工程师）

电子邮件： md@zebafoundation.in / yoursocialmd@gmail.com



原文:

Hello,
I hope you are doing well.

I'm MD Sarfaraj, and I'm a software engineer and blogger.
Since the tragic death of my sister Zeba Khatoon, I have always wondered what I could do for her. As a memorial, I established the Zeba Foundation in her honor.

Zeba Foundation plans to open a computer center in Bihar, India, to teach computer skills to underprivileged students.

In Bihar, India, many students want to learn computer skills, but are unable to afford them because of poverty. Additionally, private coaching centers charge excessive fees.

The Bihar government claims they are teaching computer skills in schools, but on the ground, nothing seems to be happening.

So let me ask you for help financially so we can open computer centers in rural areas and teach computer skills to unprivileged students.

Please visit the website for more information:
https://zebafoundation.in/

Warm regards, MD Sarfaraj (Software Engineer)

Email: md@zebafoundation.in / yoursocialmd@gmail.com

item.title: 函数式编程在真实环境中有什么优势吗？ | Does functional programming have any advantage in real environments?
item.description:
我一直在考虑学习一些新东西，所以我去了Stack Overflow 调查，看看人们现在实际在使用哪些技术，我看到了这个：

收入最高的编程语言是 Clojure？ 紧随其后的是 F#、Elixir 和 Erlang，以及 Scala 和 LISP 之后的几个位置，给我留下了深刻的印象。

我几乎没有函数式编程方面的知识，我听说在某些情况下（比如管理大数据）有更好的性能，更可预测，更易读，但我没有看到函数式编程的实际用例而不是传统的方法。

功能范式的优点是什么？ 在什么情况下使用这种语言？ 对于想要学习新事物的人，您建议先学习什么语言？ 我想到了 Haskell 或 Clojure。

谢谢！ 🙌 🙌



原文:

I've been thinking about learning something new, so I went to Stack Overflow Surveys to see what technologies are people actually working on right now, and I saw this:

The top paying programming language is Clojure? Followed by F#, Elixir and Erlang, and a few positions below Scala and LISP, I'm impressed.

I don't have almost any knowledge in functional programming, I've heard that has better performance in certain situations (like managing big data), is more predictable, and more readable, but I don't see real use cases for functional programming instead of a traditional approach.

What are the advantages of the functional paradigm? In what situations are this languages used? For someone that wants to learn something new, what language do you recommend to learn first? I thought about Haskell or Clojure.

Thanks! 🙌
item.author: alesbe

item.title: JavaScript中的空/未定义/空字符串？ | empty/undefined/null string in JavaScript?
item.description:
贡萨洛·巴雷拉

发表于前几天解决了一个bug，发现了一个很behabiorin avalidation，。

有人试图验证一个对象不是用 ! 感叹号），让我们看看在这些情况下会发生什么。

什么是感叹号？ 只是反转值的逻辑（真或假）状态。

例如

 setTimeout(() => { if (!window.mountConfig) return if (!window.Visualization) return window.mountConfig(window.Visualization) }, 5000)

在这种情况下，行
window.mountConfig(window.Visualization)
被执行！

但是关于

 setTimeout(() => { if (window.mountConfig !== 'undefined' ) return if (window.Visualization !== 'undefined' ) return window.mountConfig(window.Visualization) }, 5000)

它没有被执行！

记住！！

在验证未定义之前验证de变量的类型，它与empty或null不同

 var firstName; var lastName = null; // Try to get non existing DOM element var comment = document.getElementById('comment'); console.log(firstName); // Print: undefined console.log(lastName); // Print: null console.log(comment); // Print: null console.log(typeof firstName); // Print: undefined console.log(typeof lastName); // Print: object console.log(typeof comment); // Print: object console.log(null == undefined) // Print: true console.log(null === undefined) // Print: false /* Since null == undefined is true, the following statements will catch both null and undefined */ if(firstName == null){ alert('Variable "firstName" is undefined.'); } if(lastName == null){ alert('Variable "lastName" is null.'); } /* Since null === undefined is false, the following statements will catch only null or undefined */ if(typeof comment === 'undefined') { alert('Variable "comment" is undefined.'); } else if(comment === null){ alert('Variable "comment" is null.'); }

请阅读
https://developer.mozilla.org/es/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/undefined https://developer.mozilla.org/es/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/undefined

Nitya Narasimhan - Nitya Narasimhan - May 14 5月14日



原文:

Gonzalo Barrera

Posted on May 19

A few days ago, solving a bug, I found a very behabiorin a validation,.

someone was trying to validate that an object was not defined by using ! exclamation mark), let's see what happens in these cases.

what does an exclamation mark? just reverses the logical (true or false) state of the value.

for example

 setTimeout(() => {
      if (!window.mountConfig) return
      if (!window.Visualization) return
      window.mountConfig(window.Visualization)
}, 5000)

In this case, the line
window.mountConfig(window.Visualization)
is executed!

but what about

 setTimeout(() => {
      if (window.mountConfig !== 'undefined' ) return
      if (window.Visualization !== 'undefined' ) return
      window.mountConfig(window.Visualization)
}, 5000)

it' not executed!

Remember!!

validate the type of de variable before validate undefined, it's not the same that empty or null

var firstName;
var lastName = null;

// Try to get non existing DOM element
var comment = document.getElementById('comment');

console.log(firstName); // Print: undefined
console.log(lastName);  // Print: null
console.log(comment);   // Print: null

console.log(typeof firstName); // Print: undefined
console.log(typeof lastName);  // Print: object
console.log(typeof comment);   // Print: object

console.log(null == undefined)  // Print: true    
console.log(null === undefined) // Print: false

/* Since null == undefined is true, the following statements will catch both null and undefined */
if(firstName == null){
    alert('Variable "firstName" is undefined.');
}    
if(lastName == null){
    alert('Variable "lastName" is null.');
}

/* Since null === undefined is false, the following statements will catch only null or undefined  */

if(typeof comment === 'undefined') {
    alert('Variable "comment" is undefined.');
} else if(comment === null){
    alert('Variable "comment" is null.');
}

please read
https://developer.mozilla.org/es/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/undefined

Nitya Narasimhan - May 14
item.author: gbarreradev

item.title: 你做的一个让你非常自豪的项目是什么？ | What's the one project made by you that you're very proud of?
item.description:
我会简短地说，你制作的一个项目是什么，你非常自豪并希望与其他开发人员分享。 为这个项目感到自豪的原因有很多，比如它可能是您使用正在学习的语言制作的第一个项目，或者是一个需要数月才能创建但您能够完成的项目！

对我来说，这个项目是OctoTastic ，它是一个用于显示 github 用户的统计数据并与其他人进行比较的网络应用程序，我为这个项目感到自豪的原因是我在制作过程中学到了很多东西，大约花了 2 周的时间才完成它这是我第一个使用 tailwind css 制作的项目。



原文:

I'll keep it short, what's the one project made by you that you're very proud of and would want to share it with other developers. There can be many reasons of being proud of the project like it could be your first project made using a language you were learning, or a project which literally took months to create but you were able to complete it!

For me that project is OctoTastic, It's a web app made to display github user's stats and compare them with others, the reason I'm proud of this project is that I learnt a lot while making it, it took around 2 weeks to complete it and it's my first project made using tailwind css.
item.author: asheeshh

item.title: 如何将 p5.js 与 TypeScript 和 webpack 一起使用 | How to use p5.js with TypeScript and webpack
item.description:
这篇文章是关于使用 webpack 和 typescript 创建 p5.js 开发的。 第 1 步：安装库

首先，创建一个项目目录并安装库。

 mkdir p5-project cd p5-project npm init -y npm i -D webpack webpack-cli webpack-dev-server typescript ts-loader @types/p5 npm i p5

删除 package.json 中的"main":"index.js"并添加"private":true

 { "name" : "p5-project" , "version" : "1.0.0" , "description" : "" , "private" : true , "scripts" : { "test" : "echo \" Error: no test specified \" && exit 1" , }, "keywords" : [], "author" : "" , "license" : "ISC" , "devDependencies" : { "@types/p5" : "^1.4.2" , "ts-loader" : "^9.3.0" , "typescript" : "^4.6.4" , "webpack" : "^5.72.1" , "webpack-cli" : "^4.9.2" , "webpack-dev-server" : "^4.9.0" }, "dependencies" : { "p5" : "^1.4.1" }
}

根据 webpack 文档，这是为了防止意外发布您的代码。

第二步：创建目录和文件

在项目目录下创建src和dist目录， webpack.config.js ， tsconfig.json和global.d.ts文件。

每个文件的编写都应参考以下内容。

webpack.config.js webpack.config.js

 const path = require ( ' path ' ); module . exports = { mode : " development " , entry : ' ./src/index.ts ' , module : { rules : [ { test : / \. tsx ? $/ , use : ' ts-loader ' , exclude : /node_modules/ , }, ], }, resolve : { extensions : [ ' .tsx ' , ' .ts ' , ' .js ' ], }, output : { filename : ' bundle.js ' , path : path . resolve ( __dirname , ' dist ' ), }, devServer : { static : ' ./dist ' , } };

tsconfig.json tsconfig.json

 { "compilerOptions" : { "outDir" : "./dist/" , "noImplicitAny" : true , "module" : "es6" , "target" : "es5" , "jsx" : "react" , "allowJs" : true , "moduleResolution" : "node" }
}

全球.d.t​​s

 import module = require ( ' p5 ' ); export = module ; export as namespace p5 ;

索引.html

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang= "en" > <head> <meta charset= "UTF-8" > <title> P5 Project </title> </head> <body> <script src= "bundle.js" ></script> </body> </html>

索引.ts

 import * as p5 from ' p5 ' ; export const sketch = ( p : p5 ) => { p . setup = () => { p . createCanvas ( 400 , 400 ); } p . draw = () => { p . background ( 220 ); p . ellipse ( 50 , 50 , 80 , 80 ); } } export const myp5 = new p5 ( sketch , document . body );

使用 p5 实例模式避免可能与您自己的函数名或其他库发生冲突。

第 3 步：添加 npm 脚本并尝试构建

在 package.json 中添加"start": "webpack serve --open"并在终端中键入npm run start 。

 { "name" : "p5-project-2" , "version" : "1.0.0" , "description" : "" , "private" : true , "scripts" : { "test" : "echo \" Error: no test specified \" && exit 1" , "start" : "webpack serve --open" //add }, "keywords" : [], "author" : "" , "license" : "ISC" , "devDependencies" : { "@types/p5" : "^1.4.2" , "ts-loader" : "^9.3.0" , "typescript" : "^4.6.4" , "webpack" : "^5.72.1" , "webpack-cli" : "^4.9.2" , "webpack-dev-server" : "^4.9.0" }, "dependencies" : { "p5" : "^1.4.1" }
}

您可以在浏览器的画布中看到圆圈。
我希望你觉得这篇文章有帮助！

快乐的创意编码！

资源参考：

肖恩·多拉基亚 | shain.find - shain.find - May 16 5月16日



原文:

This post is about creating a p5.js development using webpack and typescript. (Please install Node.js beforehand and make sure npm is available.)

Step 1: Install libraries

First, create a project directory and install libraries.

mkdir p5-project
cd p5-project
npm init -y
npm i -D webpack webpack-cli webpack-dev-server typescript ts-loader @types/p5
npm i p5

Delete "main":"index.js" in package.json and add "private":true

{ "name": "p5-project", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", "private": true, "scripts": { "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1", }, "keywords": [], "author": "", "license": "ISC", "devDependencies": { "@types/p5": "^1.4.2", "ts-loader": "^9.3.0", "typescript": "^4.6.4", "webpack": "^5.72.1", "webpack-cli": "^4.9.2", "webpack-dev-server": "^4.9.0" }, "dependencies": { "p5": "^1.4.1" }
}

According to webpack documentation, this is to prevent an accidental publication of your code.

Step 2: Create directories and files

Create src and dist directories, webpack.config.js, tsconfig.json and global.d.ts files under the project directory.

The dist directory contains index.html, and the src directory contains index.ts.

p5-project
  |- package.json
  |- package-lock.json
  |- webpack.config.js
  |- tsconfig.json
  |- /dist
    |- index.html
  |- /src
    |- index.ts

Each file should be written with reference to the following.

webpack.config.js

const path = require('path'); module.exports = { mode: "development", entry: './src/index.ts', module: { rules: [ { test: /\.tsx?$/, use: 'ts-loader', exclude: /node_modules/, }, ], }, resolve: { extensions: ['.tsx', '.ts', '.js'], }, output: { filename: 'bundle.js', path: path.resolve(__dirname, 'dist'), }, devServer: { static: './dist', }
};

tsconfig.json

{ "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "./dist/", "noImplicitAny": true, "module": "es6", "target": "es5", "jsx": "react", "allowJs": true, "moduleResolution": "node" }
}

global.d.ts

import module = require('p5');
export = module;
export as namespace p5;

index.html

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <title>P5 Project</title>
</head>
<body> <script src="bundle.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

index.ts

import * as p5 from 'p5'; export const sketch = (p: p5) => { p.setup = () => { p.createCanvas(400, 400); } p.draw = () => { p.background(220); p.ellipse(50,50,80,80); }
} export const myp5 = new p5(sketch, document.body);

Using the p5 instance mode to avoid possible conflicts with your own function names or other libraries.

Step 3: Add npm scripts and try to build

Add "start": "webpack serve --open" in package.json and type npm run start in a terminal.

{ "name": "p5-project-2", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", "private": true, "scripts": { "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1", "start": "webpack serve --open" //add }, "keywords": [], "author": "", "license": "ISC", "devDependencies": { "@types/p5": "^1.4.2", "ts-loader": "^9.3.0", "typescript": "^4.6.4", "webpack": "^5.72.1", "webpack-cli": "^4.9.2", "webpack-dev-server": "^4.9.0" }, "dependencies": { "p5": "^1.4.1" }
}

You can see the circle in the canvas on the browser.
I hope you find this article helpful!

Happy Creative Coding!

item.title: #PureCSS 心 | #PureCSS Heart
item.description:
使用纯 CSS 可以实现像心形这样的形状，因为它在顶部包含弯曲的圆角，在底部包含斜线。

让我们逐步了解如何创建它

创建一个简单的方形容器（ 50px X 50px ）
将容器旋转45 度。 这将使底部倾斜线。

现在为了制作心形的两条上部曲线，我们可以使用伪前后元素。

创建边界半径为 50% 的相同大小的伪元素。 现在这些元素将不可见，因为它隐藏在 Heart 容器下。

为了使其可见，请更改相应伪元素的顶部和左侧位置。

  • 留给前一个元素
  • 后元素的顶部位置

CSS is the foundation of webpages, is used for webpage development by styling websites and web apps.You can learn How To Draw CSS Shapes from the ground up by following these CSS Examples . CSS 是网页的基础，通过样式化网站和 Web 应用程序用于网页开发。您可以按照这些CSS 示例从头开始学习如何绘制 CSS 形状。



原文:

Shapes like heart are achievable using pure CSS because it contains curved rounded corners at the top and slanting lines in the bottom.

Lets get into how we can create it step by step

Create a simple square container (50px X 50px)
rotate the container 45deg. This will make the bottom slanting lines.

Now in-order to make the two upper curves in the heart shape, we can make use of pseudo before and after element.

Create the same size pseudo element with border radius 50%. Now these element won’t be visible because its hidden under the Heart container.

In order to make it visible, change the top and left position for the respective psuedo elements.

  • left for the before element
  • top position for the after element

CSS is the foundation of webpages, is used for webpage development by styling websites and web apps.You can learn How To Draw CSS Shapes from the ground up by following these CSS Examples.

item.title: 关于 Docker 第 1 部分 | All about Docker Part 1
item.description:
发表于在 EE 中，您可以获得支持和更多产品。

查看定价： https ://www.docker.com/pricing/

安装 Docker

如果您还没有安装 Docker，Docker 已经有一些很好的指南来说明如何安装，但是如果您已经知道要安装哪个操作系统，下面是下载它的更快步骤。 本讲座之后的视频是为每个操作系统安装 Docker、然后获取 GitHub 存储库、获取代码编辑器以及根据需要调整命令行的演练。 如果您安装了 Docker Desktop 和 Visual Studio Code，请随意跳过本节的任何内容。

我总是推荐 Visual Studio Code (vscode) 作为 Docker、Kubernetes 和 DevOps 的编辑器。 它对所有操作系统都是免费的，并且有插件可以帮助您使用本课程中的许多工具。

在 Windows 10 和 Windows 11（任何版本）上安装

这是使用WSL2的 Windows 上的最佳体验。 如果您是 WSL2 的新手，这是在 Windows 上运行 Linux 的最佳方式，Docker Desktop 将引导您完成启用它。 从技术上讲，Docker 仍然可以使用“Windows 容器”在 Hyper-V 上工作，但是本课程的大部分内容都集中在 WSL2 擅长的 Linux 容器上。

在安装Docker Desktop for Windows之后。 我建议为 WSL2 发行版安装一个 Windows Store Ubuntu并在Windows 终端中使用它的 shell 是最好的 CLI 体验！ 确保检查您的 Docker Desktop 设置以提供足够的资源并为 Docker Desktop 启用所有 WSL2 发行版。

在 Windows 7 或 Windows 8 上安装

不幸的是，微软的 Do​​cker Desktop 操作系统功能在这些旧版本中不起作用。 您需要使用 Hyper-V 或安装 VirtualBox 并手动设置 Linux VM。 它比其他选项更复杂，所以我经常推荐更简单的选项：使用带有我的优惠券代码https://m.do.co/c/b813dfcad8d4的 DigitalOcean 云服务器，在 60 天内为您提供 100 美元的积分。

在 Mac 上安装

您需要安装Docker Desktop for Mac ，这很棒。

在 Linux 上安装

不要使用像apt/yum install docker.io这样的内置默认包，因为这些包是旧的，而不是官方 Docker-Built 包。

我更喜欢使用Docker 的自动化脚本来添加他们的存储库并安装所有依赖项： curl -sSL https://get.docker.com/ | sh 与 Docker 一起玩

也许您没有本地管理员，或者您的机器没有足够的资源。 好吧，这里最好的免费选择是使用play-with-docker.com ，它将在您的浏览器中运行一个或多个 Docker 实例，并为您提供一个终端来使用它。 您可以在其上创建多台机器，甚至使用 URL 以一种协作体验与他人共享会话。 我强烈建议您检查一下。 本课程的大部分讲座都可以与“PWD”一起使用，但最大的限制是它会在 4 小时后重置，届时它会删除你的服务器。

使用数字海洋

如果您使用 DigitalOcean（我最喜欢的“更简单”的云）注册一个新帐户，您可以使用我的注册积分使用较小的服务器数月。 通过上面的 Linux 链接在云端安装 Docker，并且在本地不需要 Docker 即可完成课程。 成千上万的学生这样做了：

检查您的 docker 版本让我们检查一下我们的 docker 版本

还要检查一些信息：

您还可以看到 docker 的所有命令：

另外，我们需要知道格式，因为实际上有很多命令：

图像与容器

  1. 图像是应用程序的二进制文件

  2. 让我们运行一个容器：

     docker container run --publish 80:80 nginx

    您可以看到实际发生的情况：

    现在转到您的浏览器并输入localhost
    你可以看到这样的东西。

    另外，打开docker hub，你可以看到类似这样的东西

    按 Ctrl + C 停止容器。

    也输入

    查看仍然存在的唯一容器 ID。 现在转到浏览器，您仍然会看到 nginx 网站。 现在输入

    您将看到容器仍在运行，并且您会注意到此容器 id 与我们在上一个命令中看到的相同。 现在让我们停止它

    docker container stop <1st few digits of the id>

    现在检查容器

    你什么也找不到。

    现在，让我们运行：

    也进入docker桌面，你会发现这个

    现在，您将找到 2 个容器。 为什么？
    因为


    我们已经使用了命令docker container run两次。 每次我们运行该命令时，它都会从映像 (nginx) 创建一个容器。 此外，您可以看到我们从未设置但 docker 自己设置的容器名称。 现在，让我们设置它并创建一个新容器

    docker container run --publish 80:80 --detach --name mitulhost nginx


    现在，让我们再次查看容器列表

    现在，让我们去 localhost 并刷新它以生成一些日志。 然后转到终端并输入

    docker container logs mitulhost


    现在，让我们删除我们的容器

    要删除一个容器，我们需要输入：

     docker container rm <1st few digits of container 1> <1st few digits of container 2> ...

    但这仅适用于停止的容器。 因为，我们有“mitulhost”容​​器已经在运行，我们可以停止它并运行它。或者，我们可以强制它被删除。

     docker container rm -f <1st few digits of container

    让我们看看我们是否还剩下任何容器

    没有！！！



原文:

Md Shahriyar Al Mustakim Mitul

Posted on May 19

Docker CE vs Docker EE
Docker CE is Community Edition which is free but EE is Enterprise Edition which is not free. In EE , you get support and more products.

Checkout the pricing: https://www.docker.com/pricing/

Installing Docker

If you don't already have Docker installed, Docker already has some great guides on how to do it, but if you already know which OS you want to install on, below are quicker steps for downloading it. The videos after this Lecture are walkthroughs of installing Docker for each OS, then getting the GitHub repo, getting a code editor, and tweaking the command line if you want to. Feel free to skip any of this Section if you have Docker Desktop and Visual Studio Code installed.

I always recommend Visual Studio Code (vscode) as your editor for all things Docker, Kubernetes, and DevOps. It's free for all OSs and has plugins to help you with many tools in this course.

Installing on Windows 10 and Windows 11 (any Edition)

https://docs.docker.com/desktop/windows/install/

This is the best experience on Windows, which uses WSL2. If you're new to WSL2, it's the best way to run Linux on Windows, and Docker Desktop will walk you through enabling it. Docker can technically still work on Hyper-V with "Windows Containers", but most of this course focuses on Linux Containers, which WSL2 is great at.

After installing Docker Desktop for Windows. I recommend installing a Windows Store Ubuntu for WSL2 distro and using its shell in Windows Terminal is the best CLI experience! Make sure to check your Docker Desktop settings to give it enough resources and enable all your WSL2 distros for Docker Desktop.

Installing on Windows 7 or Windows 8

Unfortunately, Microsoft's OS features for Docker Desktop don't work in these older versions. You'll need to use Hyper-V or install VirtualBox and manually setup a Linux VM. It's more involved than the other options, so I often recommend the easier option: using a DigitalOcean cloud server with my coupon code https://m.do.co/c/b813dfcad8d4 gets you $100 in credits over 60 days.

Installing on Mac

https://docs.docker.com/desktop/mac/install/

You'll want to install Docker Desktop for Mac, which is great.

Installing on Linux

https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/

Do not use your built-in default packages like apt/yum install docker.io because those packages are old and not the Official Docker-Built packages.

I prefer to use Docker's automated script to add their repository and install all dependencies: curl -sSL https://get.docker.com/ | sh but you can also install in a more manual method by following specific instructions that Docker provides for your Linux distribution.

Use Play With Docker

https://labs.play-with-docker.com/

Maybe you don't have local admin, or maybe your machine doesn't have enough resources. Well, the best free option here is to use play-with-docker.com, which will run one or more Docker instances inside your browser, and give you a terminal to use it with. You can create multiple machines on it, and even use the URL to share the session with others in a sort of collaborative experience. I highly recommend you check it out. Most of the lectures in this course can be used with "PWD", but the big limitation is it'll reset after 4 hours, at which time it'll delete your servers.

Use DigitalOcean

If you signup for a new account with DigitalOcean, my favorite "simpler" cloud, you can use a smaller server for months with my signup credit. Install Docker in the Cloud, via the above Linux links, and do the course without needing Docker locally. Thousands of students have done this:

https://m.do.co/c/b813dfcad8d4 gets you $100 in credits over 60 days

Check your docker version Let' check our docker version

Also check some information:

You may also see all the commands of docker:

Also, we need to know the formats because there are actually lots of commands:

Image vs Container

  1. Image is binaries of applications

  2. Container means the running image

Let's run a container:

docker container run --publish 80:80 nginx

You can see what actually happened:

Now go to your browser and type localhost
You can see something like this.

Also, open the docker hub and you can see something like this

Press Ctrl + C to stop the container.

Also type

to see the unique container ID which is still there . Now go to browser and you will still see the nginx website. Now type

You will see the containers still running and you will notice that this container id is same as we saw from the last command. Now lets stop it

docker container stop <1st few digits of the id>

now check the containers

You will find nothing.

Now, lets run:

Also into the docker desktop, you will find this

Now, you will find 2 containers. Why?
Because


We have used the command docker container run twice . Each time we run that command, it creates a container from the image (nginx) . Also, you can see Container name which we never set but docker did by itself. Now, lets set that and create a new container

docker container run --publish 80:80 --detach --name  mitulhost nginx


Now, lets see the container list again

Now, lets go to the localhost and refresh it to generate some logs . Then go to the terminal and them type

docker container logs mitulhost


Now, lets delete our containers

H To remove a container , we nee to type:

docker container rm <1st few digits of container  1> <1st few digits of container 2> ...

But this only works for the stopped container. As, we have "mitulhost" container already running, we can stop it and run it.Or,else we can force it to be removed.

docker container rm -f <1st few digits of container

Let's see if we have any containers left

Nothing!!!

item.description:
华丽的

发表于使用干净/洋葱代码架构在基础层（数据模型 - 也称为存储库）中提取、转换和加载数据，然后将更改广播到视图模型，这就像一个聚合层（为了表示而展平）。 这个视图模型层然后广播到 UI 层，在那里它以组件（React）的形式呈现。

铁砧工程 - May 5 5月5日



原文:

bronifty

Posted on May 19

Github Repo

Youtube Video

Using clean / onion code architecture to extract, transform and load data in a base layer (data model - aka repository), then to broadcast changes to a view model, which is like an aggregate layer (to flatten for presentation). This view model layer then broadcasts to the UI layer, where it is presented in components (React).

Anvil Engineering - May 5
item.author: bronifty

item.title: 帖子、评论、评论……表格？ | Posts, Comments, Reviews...Forms?
item.description:
我正在学习成为一名软件开发人员，作为一名程序员，我正在揭露网络的“粉红大象”； 也就是说，我们在网上输入数据的几乎所有东西，很可能都是一种形式。 希望您和我一样对该声明感到好奇，并决定检查其有效性。 我怀疑我正在输入的这个文本区域是一个表格； 我没有错。

自己检查一下：对于 Chrome 浏览器，右键单击屏幕，选择“检查”，然后在右侧显示一个带有大量代码的新面板。 滚动浏览所有代码，您应该会看到一个

里面有一个类的标签。 更准确地说，此字段是表单的<textarea>部分。

您创建的所有这些帖子和推文，无论是在时间线上、评论、回复、“联系我们”、个人资料创建和在线应用程序等等，几乎所有您输入数据的东西都是形成😱。

对于有经验的开发人员和编码人员来说，这对你来说可能不是什么新鲜事，但对于我们这些新手（或者它是菜鸟？），我认为它值得一试如何构建一个典型的基本形式。 这只是最基本的（HTML 部分）； 表单的实际功能（JavaScript 部分）将在另一个博客中。

用 HTML 构建表单是如此基本和简单，我们每天都在使用它们。 这有点涉及，但并不难 - 不是真的很难😅。 你决定。

让我们从元素标签开始
全部做完！ 不用等，这还不是。

花点时间考虑一下您希望它成为哪种形式； 这个表格的目的是什么？ 您希望允许输入的数据类型。 提前计划好这一点很重要，以了解表单元素中需要哪些附加元素和属性。

我将演示一个“简单”的联系表格，其中包括一个字段：姓名、电话、电子邮件和消息，如果您不太累，发送（提交）表格的按钮（当然，一旦它起作用了😁 ) 将被编码。

以下是我们将用于完成此任务的元素： <form>, <input> <label> <textarea>

在两个表单标签之间，是我们输入上面列出的其他元素的地方。 该元素包含一个type属性，该属性告诉计算机该字段中将有什么样的“输入”（或数据），或者数据将如何显示和表现🤓）。

以下是格式和语法： <input type= "text">这允许此输入字段的单行文本，最多包含大约 20 个字符。 让我们完成表单的 Name 字段代码的<input>部分。

这是完整的输入代码行的样子：
<form> <input type= "text" id="name" name="user_name">

添加了一个 id 以便在需要时轻松选择此输入元素，并且 name 属性在表单提交后立即定义属性类型，所以我们有点需要它🤓）。 此外，输入是一个空元素——它是自闭合的，不需要闭合标签。

我们的 Name: 字段需要另一行代码。 每个表单都会在屏幕上显示一个标签，这就是标签元素的作用。 它使用属性“for”，它可以从输入标签中获取 id 的值：

让我们插入要在屏幕上显示的文本标签，然后关闭元素。

<label for="name">Your Name:<label>

对于 Name: 字段，我们的代码是：
<form> <label for="name">Your Name:<label> <input type= "text" id="name" name="name">

如果您只需要单行名称字段，则重复上述操作即可。 为了正确显示编码人员对表单创建和接受数据类型的控制，我选择了电子邮件、电话和消息来演示需要进行哪些更改。

电子邮件：字段代码如下所示：
<label for="emailAddy">Your Email:<label>
<input type="email" id="emailAddy" name="your_email">

你能发现差异吗？ 由于下一个字段用于电子邮件，因此类型必须是电子邮件，这设置了仅在此字段中输入电子邮件地址的标准。 让我们想一想：您是否曾经错误地（或没有😈）输入了您的家庭住址而不是电子邮件，或者输入的电子邮件格式不正确，并且由于错误而无法继续填写表格？

这有助于用户输入表单主人（我刚刚做了那个标题🥸）想要的正确数据类型和格式。

我觉得读这个的人很聪明； 并且已经弄清楚电话的类型值是什么。 但生活总是充满惊喜......所以我会说你“亲密”。

<input type="tel" id="phone" name="phone">

因为从技术上讲，电话这个词是“电话”的缩写🤓，因此是“电话”。 这段代码还缺少一件事； 我们要求我们的用户输入数字，而不是文本。 我们要确保在该字段中只接受数字而不是字母。 此级别的控制需要“模式”属性：
<input type="tel" id="phone" name="phone" pattern="[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}">

当然，我们需要电话号码，并且使用模式属性，编码器正在确定什么是可以接受的。 括号[] 内的数值 0-9 是可以接受的。 在大括号 {} 中，我们指出该集合中允许有多少位数字，这里我们说只有 3 位数字是可以接受的。 然后我们有一个破折号 - ，表示该字符在电话号码格式中也是可以接受的。 在下一组 {} 中仅允许 3 位数字，然后是另一个 - ，在最后一组 {} 中仅允许 4 位数字。

这是您控制表单输入的方式。以上也是“州”中使用的电话号码格式，因此请根据您的目标用户和/或国家代码进行调整。

此外，“模式”可用作确保您希望用户使用的正确值是仅被接受的一种方式，您作为编码器/开发人员/表单管理员可以根据您在“”中放置的内容进行控制（引用）属性模式。

现在，让我们通过添加电话字段的标签来结束本节。
<label for="phone">Your Phone Number:</label>

呼，这有点吧？ 不？ 好的！ 这是构建此表单的最后一部分，即消息框。 让我们直接跳进去！
<textarea id="msgArea" name="usersMsg"></textarea>
<label for="msgArea">Your Message:</label>

你注意到了吗？ 请记住，输入元素只允许一行文本和大约 20 个字符。 消息框允许更多行的文本和字符。 此外，textarea 元素需要一个结束标记。 除此之外，其他一切都应该看起来很熟悉。

如果您一直在编码，或者决定滚动到最后的最终产品🤭，这里有货：

<form> <label for="name">Your Name:</label> <input type= "text" id="name" name="name"> <label for="emailAddy">Your Email:</label> <input type="email" id="emailAddy" name="your_email"> <label for="phone">Your Phone Number:</label> <input type="tel" id="phone" name="phone" pattern="[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}"> <label for="msgArea">Your Message:</label> <textarea id="msgArea" name="usersMsg"></textarea>

最后，请注意 label 总是在 input 之上，我不会告诉你为什么，但可以玩一下看看。 表单的布局、属性和功能太多了，这几乎不是一种味道。 我使用的一些资源包括W3Schools 、 MDN ，当您准备好为表单设置样式时，请查看CSS Grid CSS Selectors

感谢您阅读和编码，我希望这对某人有所帮助！😁



原文:

I am studying to become a Software Developer, and as a coder I am exposing the 'pink elephant' of the web; which is that almost everything we input data into online, is most likely a form. Hopefully, you are curious about that statement just as I was, and decided to check its validity. I suspected that this very text area I am typing into, is a form; I was not wrong.

Check for yourself: for Chrome browsers, right click on the screen, select "inspect", and to the right, a new panel displays with a lot of code. Scrolling through all the code, you should see a

tag that has a class inside of it. To be more precise, this field is the <textarea> section of the form.

All those posts, and tweets you created, be it on a timeline, reviews, replies, the "contact us", the profile creations, and online applications to name a few, almost all of the things you have input data into have been a form 😱.

For experienced developers and coders, this may not be anything shockingly new to you, but for us newbies (or is it noobs?), I think its worth a walk through on how to construct a typical basic form. This will be just be the bare bones (HTML portion); the actual functionality(JavaScript part) of a form will be in another blog.

Constructing a form in HTML is something so basic and simple, we use them daily. It is a bit involving, but not hard--well not really hard 😅. You decide.

Let's start with the element tag
<form> </form> and ...ta daaa! All Done! No wait, that's not quite it.

Take a moment to think about what kind of form you want this to be; what is the purpose of this form? The type of data you want to allow to be entered. It's important to plan this out in advance to know what additional elements and attributes will be needed within the form element.

I will demo a "simple" contact form that will include a field for : Name, Phone, Email, and Message, and if you aren't too tired, the button to send(submit)the form (once it functional of course😁)will be coded.

Here are the elements we will use to get this task done: <form>, <input> <label> <textarea>

Between the two form tags, is where we type the other elements listed above. The element contains a type attribute which tells the computer what kind of 'input' (or data) will be in this field, or how the data will appear and behave 🤓).

Here's the format and syntax: <input type= "text"> this allows a single line of text for this input field, that consist of about 20 max characters. Let's finish off the <input> part of the code for the Name field of our form.

This is what the completed input line of code looks like:
<form> <input type= "text" id="name" name="user_name">

</form>

An id was added to easily select this input element when needed, and the name attribute defines the attribute type as soon as the form is submitted, so we kinda of need it 🤓). Also, input is an empty element--its self closing and doesn't require a closing tag.

Theres another line of code needed for our Name: field. Every form displays a label on screen, and that is what the label element does. It uses the attribute "for", which can take the value of the id from the input label:

<label for="name"> Since label is not an empty element, it requires a closing tag. Let's insert the text label we want to display on the screen, and close out the element.

<label for="name">Your Name:<label>

All together for the Name: field, our code is:
<form> <label for="name">Your Name:<label> <input type= "text" id="name" name="name">

</form>

If you just want single line name fields, then repeating the above will do. To properly show the control a coder has with form creating and acceptance of data type, I selected email, phone and message to demonstrate what changes are needed.

The Email: field code looks like this:
<label for="emailAddy">Your Email:<label>
<input type="email" id="emailAddy" name="your_email">

Can you spot the differences? Since this next field is for email, the type has to be email, which sets the standard of only an email address to be entered into this field. Let's think for a moment: Have you ever mistakenly(or not 😈) typed your home address instead of email, or typed anything other than the correct email format, and couldn't proceed with form completion due to an error?

This helps the user enter the correct data type and format of what the form master (I just made that title up 🥸)wants.

I feel that the person reading this is quite intelligent; and has already figured out what the type value will be for phone. But life always full of surprises...so I will say that you were "close".

<input type="tel" id="phone" name="phone">

Because technically, the word phone is short for "telephone" 🤓 hence, 'tel'. And there is one more thing missing from this code; we are asking our user to input numbers, not text. And we want to make sure that only numbers, and not alphabets are accepted inside this field. This level of control requires the 'pattern' attribute:
<input type="tel" id="phone" name="phone" pattern="[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}">

Of course we want numbers for a phone , and with the pattern attribute, the coder is establishing what is acceptable. Inside the brackets[]'s numeric values 0-9 are acceptable. And inside of the curly braces {}, we indicate how many digits in this set is allowed, here we are saying only 3 digits are acceptable. Then we have a dash - , to indicate that character is also acceptable in the phone number format. In the next set of {}'s only 3 digits will be allowed, followed by another - , and in the last set of {}'s only 4 digits will be allowed.

This is how you control the form's input.The above is also the phone number format used in the 'States, so adjust according to your target users and/or country code.

Additionally, 'pattern' can be used as a way to ensure that the proper values you want the user to use is what is only accepted, you as the coder/developer/form master have that control based on what you place inside the " " (quotations) of the attribute pattern.

Now, let's finish off this section with adding the label for the phone field.
<label for="phone">Your Phone Number:</label>

Whew, this is a bit huh? No? Good! Here is the last part of constructing this form, the message box. Let's just jump right in!
<textarea id="msgArea" name="usersMsg"></textarea>
<label for="msgArea">Your Message:</label>

Did you notice it? Remember that the input element only allows one line of text, and about 20 characters. A message box allows more lines of text and characters. Also, the textarea element requires a closing tag. Aside from that, everything else should look familiar.

If you have been coding along, or decided to scroll to the end for the final product 🤭, here are the goods:

<form> <label for="name">Your Name:</label> <input type= "text" id="name" name="name"> <label for="emailAddy">Your Email:</label> <input type="email" id="emailAddy" name="your_email"> <label for="phone">Your Phone Number:</label> <input type="tel" id="phone" name="phone" pattern="[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}"> <label for="msgArea">Your Message:</label> <textarea id="msgArea" name="usersMsg"></textarea>

</form>

Lastly, notice that label is always above input, I wont tell you why, but play around with it and see. There are so many layouts and attributes and functionality of forms, this was barely a taste. Some resources I use include W3Schools, MDN, and when you're ready to style your forms, check out CSS Grid CSS Selectors

Thanks for reading and coding along, I hope this helped someone!😁

item.title: 第564天：我仍然相信 | Day 564 : I Still Believe
item.description:
嘻哈和代码

发表于班轮注意事项：

  • 专业人士：美好的一天。 有我录制视频的会议，它进行得非常好。 人们告诉我他们真的很喜欢它。 我仍然相信它让人们了解了我们在项目中的进展情况以及我们未来的发展方向。 开了一些会，把一些项目从我的旧笔记本电脑推送到 GitHub，这样我就可以在我的新笔记本电脑上处理它们了。

  • 个人：昨晚，基本上只是在录制今天的视频。 我的工作笔记本电脑运行得很慢。 在那之后，看了一集亚特兰大，对我的下一个项目做了一些思考。 然后就去睡觉了。

不知道时间都去哪儿了。 我记得的最后一件事是把我的项目放到 GitHub 上，接下来我知道是晚上了，我还没有写这篇博文。 哈哈。 可能会为我的业余项目研究一些东西，然后就结束了。 我也真的需要在广播节目上工作。

有一个美好的夜晚！

和平片 Dwane / conshus

比利·拉米雷斯—— 丹尼斯墨菲—— Jun 3 '21 21 年 6 月 3 日



原文:

HIPHOP and CODE

Posted on May 19

liner notes:

  • Professional : Pretty good day. Had the meeting that I recorded the video for and it went really well. People told me they really liked it. I still believe it gave folks a good update on where we are with the project and where we are going in the future. Had some more meetings and got some projects pushed from my old laptop to GitHub so I can work on them on my new laptop.

  • Personal : Last night, basically just worked on recording the video for today. My work laptop was running pretty slow. After that, watched an episode of Atlanta and did some thinking on my next side project. Then went to sleep.

No clue where the time went. Last thing I remember was getting my projects up on GitHub and the next thing I know it's night time and I haven't written this blog post. haha. Probably look into some stuff for my side project and call it a night. I really need to work on the radio show also.

Have a great night!

peace piece Dwane / conshus

https://dwane.io / https://HIPHOPandCODE.com

Billi Ramirez - Jul 13 '21

Linx Software - Jul 13 '21

Dennis Murphy - Jun 3 '21

理查德·伦伯特

发表于画一个简单的矩形。 您选择的矩形的高度和宽度将决定椭圆的大小和形状。

边界半径是指形状拐角处的曲率； 它应该设置为一个非常高的值（50% 到 100%）。

画一个圆（椭圆的特殊情况）保持高度和宽度相等。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

Richard Rembert

Posted on May 19

Draw a simple rectangle. Your choice of height and width, of the rectangle, will dictate the size and shape of the ellipse.

The border-radius refers to the curvature at the corners of the ​shape; it should be set to a very high value (50% to 100%).

To draw a circle (a special case of ellipses) keep the height and width equal.

item.title: 获得报酬 IRL：如何使用 Stripe 终端收款（第 1/4 部分） | Get paid IRL: How to accept money with Stripe Terminal (Part 1/4)
item.description:
您知道您可以使用Stripe接受当面付款吗？

在本系列中，我们将一头扎进使用Stripe 终端和WisePOS E 读卡器构建个人支付网络应用程序。

在本系列中，我们将使用仅 API 服务器驱动的集成路径进行集成。

在这篇就职文章中，我们将介绍第一步：将 Stripe 终端阅读器注册到您的帐户。

手头没有 WisePOS E 读卡器？ 不用担心！ 我们将介绍如何设置用于测试和开发的模拟阅读器。 想用实物进行测试吗？ 您可以从Stripe Dashboard 的终端商店订购读卡器。

在本教程中，我们将介绍如何为 Stripe 终端注册读卡器和模拟读卡器。 为简单起见，我们将使用 Stripe Dashboard 来注册我们的读者，但您也可以使用 Stripe Terminal 的 API来构建您自己的注册流程。

要将读卡器注册到您的 Stripe 帐户，您需要执行两个步骤：

创建位置

导航到您的 Stripe Dashboard 并通过单击导航菜单中的More和Terminal转到Terminal 仪表板。

系统可能会提示您一些有关终端的定价信息。 在您开始接受实时付款之前，您不会被收取任何费用。 在本系列中，我们只会在测试模式下做事。

在您的帐户处于测试模式时，导航到位置并创建一个位置。 位置用于将读者分组到一个物理位置。 将阅读器分配给位置可以轻松过滤和检索正确商店的正确阅读器。 它们还用于配置阅读器，因此必须为每个阅读器分配一个位置。

要创建位置，请单击位置部分中的+新建，然后输入位置的名称和地址信息。

下一步是将 WisePOS E 阅读器注册到您新创建的位置。

将读卡器注册到位置

服务器驱动的集成仅与BBPOS WisePOS E兼容，因此我们需要注册该特定阅读器的物理或模拟。

您可以通过单击Readers附近的+New将设备注册到位置。

如果您有 WisePOS E，则需要将其打开并从“设置”中将其连接到 WiFi。 请记住，WiFi必须使用带有密码保护的 WPA-Personal 或 WPA2-Personal 加密。 企业和不安全的网络与 Stripe 终端不兼容。

一旦您的 WisePOS E 连接到 WiFi，如果您的阅读器是未注册的设备，您的阅读器将自动显示其注册码。

您也可以通过在设备上向左滑动打开设置菜单并单击注册码来获取设备的注册码。

无论哪种情况，请在注册码字段中输入您在设备上看到的注册码。

模拟 WisePOS E

如果您没有阅读器，您可以使用模拟的 WisePOS E 进行测试和开发，方法是输入simulated-wpe作为注册码。

无论您设置的是模拟还是真实的 WisePOS E，您现在都应该会看到一个模拟或物理设备注册到您的新位置。 对于 Stripe 的服务器驱动集成，这就是设置阅读器所需的全部内容。

下一步：检索和渲染 Stripe 终端阅读器

在我们的下一篇文章中，我们将学习如何从 Stripe 的终端阅读器 API中检索我们的阅读器并将它们呈现在一个网络应用程序中，以便我们可以提示他们收集当面付款。

保持联系

想要了解 Stripe 的最新集成、功能和开源项目吗？

关于作者

在业余时间，他喜欢画画、玩游戏和重温《权力的游戏》前五季。



原文:

Did you know that you can accept in-person payments with Stripe?

In this series, we’re going to dive headfirst into building an in-person payments web app using Stripe Terminal and the WisePOS E card reader.

Stripe Terminal is a set of APIs and SDKs that allow you to process payments with one of Stripe’s supported card readers and Stripe’s core payment APIs. In this series, we’ll be integrating using the API-only server-driven integration path.

In this inaugural post, we walk through the first step: registering a Stripe Terminal reader to your account.

Don’t have a WisePOS E card reader on hand? No worries! We’ll cover how to set up a simulated reader for testing and development. Want to test with the real thing? You can order a card reader from your Stripe Dashboard’s Terminal shop.

In this tutorial, we’ll walk through registering a card reader and simulated card reader for Stripe Terminal. For simplicity’s sake, we’ll be using the Stripe Dashboard to register our readers, but you can use Stripe Terminal’s APIs to build your own registration flow as well.

In order to register a card reader to your Stripe account, you need to take two steps:

Creating a Location

Navigate to your Stripe Dashboard and go to the Terminal dashboard by clicking More in the navigation menu and then Terminal.

You may be prompted with some pricing information about Terminal. You won’t be charged anything until you start taking live payments. In this series, we’ll only be doing things in test mode.

With your account in Test mode, navigate to Locations and create a Location. Locations are used to group readers to a physical place. A Stripe Terminal integration could include hundreds of readers across multiple stores. Assigning a reader to a Location makes it easy to filter and retrieve the right readers for the right store. They’re also used to configure the reader, so every reader must be assigned to a Location.

To create a Location click +New in the Locations section and enter the location’s Name and Address information.

The next step is registering a WisePOS E reader to your newly created Location.

Registering a card reader to a Location

The server-driven integration is only compatible with the BBPOS WisePOS E, so we'll want to register a physical or simulated of that specific reader.

💡 If you want to integrate with the other readers, you’ll need to use Stripe Terminal’s iOS, Android, or JavaScript SDK.

You can register a device to a Location by clicking +New near Readers.

WisePOS E

If you have a WisePOS E, you’ll want to turn it on and connect it to WiFi from the Settings. Keep in mind that the WiFi must use WPA-Personal or WPA2-Personal encryption with password protection. Enterprise and unsecured networks aren’t compatible with Stripe Terminal.

Once your WisePOS E has been connected to WiFi, your reader will automatically show its registration code if it's an unregistered device.

You can also get the device’s registration code by swiping left on the device to open the Settings menu and clicking Registration Code.

In either case, type the registration code you see on the device in the Registration code field.

Simulated WisePOS E

If you don’t have a reader, you can use a simulated WisePOS E for testing and development by inputting simulated-wpe as the registration code.

Whether you’ve set up a simulated or real WisePOS E, you should now see a simulated or physical device registered to your new location. For Stripe’s server-driven integration, this is all that’s required to setup the reader.

Next up: Retrieving and rendering Stripe Terminal Readers

In our next post, we’ll learn how to retrieve our readers from Stripe's Terminal Reader API and render them in a web app so that we can prompt them to collect in-person payments.

Stay connected

Want to stay up to date on Stripe’s latest integrations, features, and open-source projects?

📣 Follow us on Twitter
💬 Join the official Discord server
📺 Subscribe to our Youtube channel
📧 Sign up for the Dev Digest

About the author

Charles Watkins is a Developer Advocate at Stripe where he writes, codes, and livestreams about online payments. In his spare time, he enjoys drawing, gaming, and rewatching the first five seasons of Game of Thrones.

item.title: 如何在 setup 函数中访问 nuxt-i18n 的本地化消息 | How to access localized messages of nuxt-i18n inside the setup function
item.description:
快速片段，登录客户端检查是否避免服务器日志被转发到浏览器控制台，它也适用于服务器端。
我写这个主要是为了记住它，我总是忘记怎么做😅️。

在 nuxt 2 项目的@nuxt-i18n 7.X 中测试

setup ( props , ctx ) { const { root } = ctx ; // all the stuff is in the root, who would imagine that ha if ( process . client ) { // ctx.root.$i18n.t console . log ( ctx . root . $i18n . t ( ' my.message.path ' )) } }

这就是在setup函数中使用$t nuxt-i18n的方法。

Ravgeet Dhillon - Ravgeet Dhillon - Dec 6 '21 21 年 12 月 6 日

Aymane Mimouni - Aymane Mimouni - Nov 28 '21 21 年 11 月 28 日



原文:

Quick snippet, log inside a client check if to avoid server logs get forwarded to browser console, it works server side too.
Im mostly writing this to rember it, i allways forget how to doit 😅️.

Tested in @nuxt-i18n 7.X on a nuxt 2 project

setup(props, ctx ) { const { root } = ctx; // all the stuff is in the root, who would imagine that ha if (process.client) { // ctx.root.$i18n.t console.log(ctx.root.$i18n.t('my.message.path')) } }

And that's how to use $t nuxt-i18n inside setup function folks.

Ravgeet Dhillon - Dec 6 '21

Aymane Mimouni - Nov 28 '21

item.title: Laravel 验证同一页面上的多个表单 | Laravel validation for multiple forms on the same page
item.description:
由 Max Chen 在 Unsplash 上拍摄

我必须在同一页面上实现两个表单，以便在Laravel 基本管理面板上更新配置文件。

一种用于基本信息，另一种用于密码更新。 对于验证，在控制器中使用了 $request->validate() 方法

$request->validate([ 'name' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'], 'email' => ['required', 'string', 'email', 'max:255', 'unique:users,email,'.\Auth::user()->id], ]);

但它在两种表单上都显示错误，因为相同的 $errors 变量用于显示错误。

变量的 $errors 将是 Illuminate\Support\MessageBag 的一个实例。 为了解决这个问题，Laravel 有内置的解决方案。 我们可以将名称添加到 MessageBag。 命名错误包

我们可以通过多种方式添加命名错误。

  • 要为错误包添加名称，只需将名称作为第二个参数传递给 withErrors

    对于帐户，添加了“帐户”作为命名错误，并为密码更新表单添加了“密码”。

     $validator = Validator::make($request->all(), [ 'name' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'], 'email' => ['required', 'string', 'email', 'max:255', 'unique:users,email,'.\Auth::user()->id], ]); if ($validator->fails()) { return redirect('admin.account.info') ->withErrors($validator, 'account') ->withInput(); }

    更新以下代码以查看错误显示

    如何在视图中显示命名错误包

    @if ($errors->account->any()) <ul class="mt-3 list-none list-inside text-sm text-red-400"> @foreach ($errors->account->all() as $error) <li>{{ $error }}</li> @endforeach </ul> @endif

    如果验证失败，我们可以使用 validateWithBag 方法将错误消息存储在一个命名的错误包中：

     \Validator::make($request->all(), [ 'name' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'], 'email' => ['required', 'string', 'email', 'max:255', 'unique:users,email,'.\Auth::user()->id], ])->validateWithBag('account');

    另外，我们可以使用 $request->validateWithBag

     $request->validateWithBag('account', [ 'name' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'], 'email' => ['required', 'string', 'email', 'max:255', 'unique:users,email,'.\Auth::user()->id], ]);

    添加了以下代码以验证密码

    $validator = \Validator::make($request->all(), [ 'old_password' => ['required'], 'new_password' => ['required', Rules\Password::defaults()], 'confirm_password' => ['required', 'same:new_password', Rules\Password::defaults()], ]); $validator->after(function ($validator) use ($request) { if ($validator->failed()) return; if (! Hash::check($request->input('old_password'), \Auth::user()->password)) { $validator->errors()->add( 'old_password', 'Old password is incorrect.' ); } }); $validator->validateWithBag('password');


    感谢您的阅读！

    添加您的反馈和评论如果错过了显示多个表单的错误消息的任何其他方式。

维多利亚·普朗克—— Mar 25 3月25日



原文:


Photo by Max Chen on Unsplash

I have to implement two forms on the same page for profile updates on the Laravel Basic Admin panel.

One form for basic info and another for password updates. For validation, used $request->validate() a method in the controller

$request->validate([
    'name' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'],
    'email' => ['required', 'string', 'email', 'max:255', 'unique:users,email,'.\Auth::user()->id],
]);

In view used the below code to display the errors.

@if ($errors->any())
    <ul class="mt-3 list-none list-inside text-sm text-red-400">
        @foreach ($errors->all() as $error)
            <li>{{ $error }}</li>
        @endforeach
    </ul>
@endif

But it displayed errors on both forms because the same $errors variable was used to display errors.

The $errors the variable will be an instance of Illuminate\Support\MessageBag. To resolve the issue, Laravel has the inbuild solution. We can add the names to MessageBag. Laravel is Named Error Bags

Named Error Bags

We can add named errors in multiple ways.

  • 1. Redirect withErrors
  • 2. validateWithBag method

1. Redirect withErrors

To add a name to the error bag, just pass a name as the second argument to withErrors

For the account added the ‘account’ as a named error and ‘password’ for the password update form.

$validator = Validator::make($request->all(), [
    'name' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'],
    'email' => ['required', 'string', 'email', 'max:255', 'unique:users,email,'.\Auth::user()->id],
]);

if ($validator->fails()) {
    return redirect('admin.account.info')
                ->withErrors($validator, 'account')
                ->withInput();
}

Update the below code for views error display

How to show named error bag on view 

@if ($errors->account->any())
    <ul class="mt-3 list-none list-inside text-sm text-red-400">
        @foreach ($errors->account->all() as $error)
            <li>{{ $error }}</li>
        @endforeach
    </ul>
@endif

2. validateWithBag method

We can use the validateWithBag method to store the error messages in a named error bag if validation fails:

\Validator::make($request->all(), [
    'name' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'],
    'email' => ['required', 'string', 'email', 'max:255', 'unique:users,email,'.\Auth::user()->id],
])->validateWithBag('account');

Also, we can use $request->validateWithBag

$request->validateWithBag('account', [
    'name' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'],
    'email' => ['required', 'string', 'email', 'max:255', 'unique:users,email,'.\Auth::user()->id],
]);

Added below code to validate the password

$validator = \Validator::make($request->all(), [
    'old_password' => ['required'],
    'new_password' => ['required', Rules\Password::defaults()],
    'confirm_password' => ['required', 'same:new_password', Rules\Password::defaults()],
]);

$validator->after(function ($validator) use ($request) {
    if ($validator->failed()) return;
    if (! Hash::check($request->input('old_password'), \Auth::user()->password)) {
        $validator->errors()->add(
            'old_password', 'Old password is incorrect.'
        );
    }
});

$validator->validateWithBag('password');


Laravel validation for multiple forms on the same page

Thank you for reading!

Add your feedback and comment If missed any other ways to display an error message for multiple forms.

Victorien Plancke - Mar 25

item.title: 数组和对象操作 + DOM 差异 | Array and Object Manipulation + DOM differences
item.description:
太长了（大约几个星期） ，每次我开始编写 For... 循环时，我都会怀疑自己。 在这里，我将向您展示如何知道应该使用哪一个来完成手头的任务，当您选择错误的时候会发生什么，以及这如何扩展到 DOM 元素。

对于...的

在处理数据数组时，使用 For...of 循环是一个不错的选择。

每次在此处进入循环时，都会将数组中的一个新条目加载到 i 的值中。 这里的第一个console.log 将显示i 等于array[0] 的值，或者'apple'。 第二个为“香蕉”，依此类推。 注意 i不等于项目的索引值。

对于...在

现在对于对象，您要使用 For...in 循环。 我喜欢想象我正在查看对象的一侧以查看所有键和值。

对于这种类型的循环，每次重新分配 i 时，它都会获得一个新的key 。 现在，让我们更改我们的 objectLogger 函数，看看我们是否可以查看这些值。

通过选择记录 object[i] 而不是 i，我们可以看到对象中的每个值。 因此，通过使用这种类型的循环，我们可以全面了解对象中的每个键值对。 访问这样的对象时要记住的重要一点是不要使用点表示法，而是使用计算成员访问运算符，也就是括号，来获取对象属性。 在这个函数中，试图 console.log 对象。 如果我们忘记了会发生什么？

在尝试将对象视为数组的情况下，将立即发生灾难性后果，并且您的 IDE 会在您尝试运行代码时立即说明这一点。

 arrayLogger ( animalObject ) // will return the following: index . js : 6 Uncaught TypeError : array is not iterable at arrayLogger ( index . js : 6 : 25 ) at HTMLDocument . < anonymous > ( index . js : 24 : 5 )

但是，在将数组视为对象的情况下，会发生一些有趣的事情。

 objectLogger ( fruitArray ) //=> apple, banana, cantaloupe

实际上，您可以将数组视为 JavaScript 中的对象，因为它是对象的子类型。

看看这个：

 console . log ( typeof fruitArray ) //=> object console . log ( typeof animalObject ) //=> object

有趣的。 这也是为什么使用typeof运算符来区分数组和对象也是一个糟糕的想法的原因。

在对漂亮的 HTML 页面进行了一些实验之后，我能够更好地了解将 HTML/Node 元素集合在一起时究竟会发生什么。

我的测试 HTML 文件的相关部分。

我会省去你令人眼花缭乱的全部输出。

那么让我们看看当我们以 HTML 集合形式收集元素时会发生什么。

 const divItems = document . getElementsByTagName ( ' div ' ) console . log ( divItems ) //=> HTMLCollection(7) [div, div, div, div, div, div, div] for ( const i of divItems ) { console . log ( i ) } // will output : // <div>Hey</div> // <div>it's</div> // <div>a</div> // <div>whole</div> // <div>bunch</div> // <div>of</div> // <div>divs!</div>

好的，所以这个 HTML 集合是一种数组。 但是如果我们把它当作一个对象来处理呢？

 for ( const i in divItems ) { console . log ( i ) } // will output the following (numbers on one line for readability) // 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 // length // item // namedItem

哇，秘密属性。 所以这就是为什么一个 HTML 集合比一个简单的数组更重要的原因。 但是，让我们进入更优秀的 querySelectorAll，看看我为什么首先提出这个要求。

 const divList = document . querySelectorAll ( ' section div ' ) console . log ( divList ) //=> NodeList(7) [div, div, div, div, div, div, div] for ( const i of divItems ) { console . log ( i ) } // will output : // <div>Hey</div> // <div>it's</div> // <div>a</div> // <div>whole</div> // <div>bunch</div> // <div>of</div> // <div>divs!</div>

好吧，到目前为止，几乎相同。 除了它被称为NodeList 。 随便吧？ 但是等一下，让我们试着把它当作一个对象来阅读。

 for ( const i in divItems ) { console . log ( i ) } // will output the following (numbers on one line for readability) // 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 // entries // keys // values // forEach // length // item

这就是我更喜欢 NodeList 的原因。 它有可供使用的 forEach 方法，因为它是从它的 Prototype 继承的（也许我稍后会写）。 如果您将 HTML/Node 集合的 console.log 作为一个整体展开并查看最后一个条目，您可以发现 For...in 循环读取的附加对象“键”。

查看我们的 HTML 集合条目。

因此，如果您的目标是遍历页面上的一堆 HTML 元素，那么您最好的办法是查询SelectorAll 那些坏男孩，然后对它们使用 For...of 循环或 forEach 方法。

当您想知道数组的第 1、2 或第 n 项时，请在数组上使用 For... of循环。 当您想查看对象中的键：值对时，请在对象上使用 For... in循环。 谢谢阅读！



原文:

For too long (about a few weeks), I have second guessed myself every time I've started writing a For... loop. Here, I will show you how to know which one you should use for the task at hand, what can happen when you choose the wrong one, and how this extends to DOM elements.

For... of

When handling arrays of data, using a For...of loop is a great option.

Every time the loop is entered here, a new entry from the array is loaded into the value of i. The first console.log here will show i as being equal to the value of array[0], or 'apple'. The second as 'banana' and so on. Note that i is not equal to the index value of the item.

For... in

Now for objects, a For...in loop is what you want to use. I like to imagine that I'm looking inside of the object to see all the keys and values.

For this type of loop, every time i is reassigned, it gets a new key. Now, let's change our objectLogger function to see if we can view the values instead.

By choosing to log object[i] instead of i, we can see every value in our object. So by using this type of loop, we can gain complete insight into every key:value pair in our object. An important thing to keep in mind when accessing objects like this is to NOT use dot notation and instead use the computed member access operator, aka brackets, for getting object properties. In this function, attempting to console.log object . i results in an undefined error as JavaScript tries to look through the object for a key literally called i.

What happens if we forget?

In the case of trying to treat an object as if it were an array, disastrous consequences will immediately occur and your IDE will make that clear as soon as you try to run the code.

arrayLogger(animalObject)
// will return the following:
index.js:6 Uncaught TypeError: array is not iterable at arrayLogger (index.js:6:25) at HTMLDocument.<anonymous> (index.js:24:5)

However, in the case of treating an array as an object, something interesting happens.

objectLogger(fruitArray)
//=> apple, banana, cantaloupe

You are actually able to treat an array as an object in JavaScript, because it is a subtype of object.

Check it out:

console.log(typeof fruitArray)
//=> object
console.log(typeof animalObject)
//=> object

Interesting. This is also why it is also a terrible idea to use the typeof operator to distinguish between an array and an object.

DOM Elements and Notes on Handling Them

After some experimentation on a beautiful HTML page, I've been able to get a better handle on what exactly happens when putting together a collection of HTML/Node elements.

The relevant portion of my test HTML file.

I'll spare you the eye-searing entirety of the output.

So let's see what happens when we gather the elements up in HTML collection form.

const divItems = document.getElementsByTagName('div')
console.log(divItems)
//=> HTMLCollection(7) [div, div, div, div, div, div, div]
for (const i of divItems) { console.log(i)
}
// will output : // <div>Hey</div> // <div>it's</div> // <div>a</div> // <div>whole</div> // <div>bunch</div> // <div>of</div> // <div>divs!</div>

Okay, so this HTML collection is a type of array. But what if we handle it like an object?

for (const i in divItems) { console.log(i)
}
// will output the following (numbers on one line for readability)
// 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6
// length
// item
// namedItem

Whoa, secret properties. So this is why an HTML collection is a bit more substantial than a mere array. But let's get into the far superior querySelectorAll to see why I make that claim in the first place.

const divList = document.querySelectorAll('section div')
console.log(divList)
//=> NodeList(7) [div, div, div, div, div, div, div]
for (const i of divItems) { console.log(i)
}
// will output : // <div>Hey</div> // <div>it's</div> // <div>a</div> // <div>whole</div> // <div>bunch</div> // <div>of</div> // <div>divs!</div>

Alright so far, pretty much the same. Except it's called a NodeList. Whatever, right? But hang on, let's try to read it like an object.

for (const i in divItems) { console.log(i)
}
// will output the following (numbers on one line for readability)
// 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6
// entries
// keys
// values
// forEach
// length
// item

And there's why I like a NodeList more. It has the forEach method available for use because it is inherited from its Prototype (maybe I'll write about that later). If you expand the console.log for the HTML/Node collection as a whole and look at the last entry, you can spot the additional object 'keys' that are read by a For...in loop.

Peering inside our HTML collection entry.

So if your goal is to iterate through bunch of HTML elements on a page, your best best is to querySelectorAll those bad boys and either use a For...of loop or a forEach method on them.

TL;DR

Use a For... of loop on an array when you want to know the 1st, 2nd, or nth item of the array. Use a For... in loop on an object when you want to see the key:value pairs inside it. HTMLCollections and NodeLists are both a type of array, but only a NodeList has access to the forEach method.

Thanks for reading!
item.author: sberdup

item.title: #PureCSS 圈子 | #PureCSS Circle
item.description:
里奇·伦伯特

发表于要创建一个圆，您可以在元素上设置边框半径。 这将在元素上创建弯曲的角。

如果将其设置为 50%，它将创建一个圆圈。
